articles.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 FE and Galaxy Tab S12 make an early appearance on the road to launch
articles.excerpt: Forget foldables: Evidence is already arriving for Samsung's next big launches
articles.body: TL;DR Samsung’s Galaxy Tab S12 and S26 FE just showed up referencened in the Google app. Google compiles a list of future devices set to arrive with a free premium Google AI trial for new owners. The new models may launch sometime this September — if not sooner. We are just days — hours, even — away from Samsung’s next Galaxy Unpacked event , when, this Wednesday, we expect to see it introduce the latest Galaxy Fold, Galaxy Flip, and Galaxy Watch models . But for as excited as we are about those launches, they aren’t the only new Samsung products currently on our radar. Looking a little further out, we’re also anticipating some new tablets with the Galaxy Tab S12 series, as well as the next addition to the Galaxy S26 family with the launch of the Galaxy S26 FE .

articles.title: HollowGraph Malware Uses Microsoft 365 Calendar Events as Dead-Drop C2 Channel
articles.excerpt: What Happened On July 20, 2026, cybersecurity firm Group-IB disclosed a new espionage implant dubbed HollowGraph that hijacks compromised Microsoft 365 mailboxes to run a command-and-control (C2) chan…
articles.body: What Happened On July 20, 2026, cybersecurity firm Group-IB disclosed a new espionage implant dubbed HollowGraph that hijacks compromised Microsoft 365 mailboxes to run a command-and-control (C2) channel hidden inside calendar events. The malware attaches encrypted files to calendar entries dated May 13, 2050 — far enough in the future that a mailbox owner would never scroll to them — and retrieves operator instructions from the same dead drop. All traffic moves through the Microsoft Graph API, making the activity indistinguishable from legitimate M365 usage. At least 12 systems have been infected, with three actively communicating with the threat actor between June 3 and July 9, 2026. The indicators point to a targeted espionage campaign focused on Israeli organizations .
articles.author: Diego Diaz

articles.title: How to Use React Native Libraries in Your Vega App
articles.excerpt: Vega is Amazon's Linux-based operating system for Fire TV. It uses a fork of React Native (React Native for Vega), so not all RN libraries work out of the box. That means if you are porting your app t…
articles.body: Vega is Amazon's Linux-based operating system for Fire TV . It uses a fork of React Native ( React Native for Vega ), so not all RN libraries work out of the box. That means if you are porting your app to Vega, you may need to wire in a Vega fork of some of your libraries as well. The good news is that we have ported many of the most popular libraries used in TV apps, and we've also built the Vega Module Resolver Preset (VMRP) , which replaces third-party libraries with their Vega-platform ported equivalencies.
articles.author: Moses Roth

articles.title: Turn DEV.to Into an AI Tool: Build Your Own MCP Server
articles.excerpt: What if your AI assistant could list your DEV Community posts, inspect an article, and prepare a new draft without you copying content between browser tabs? That is exactly the kind of workflow the Mo…
articles.body: What if your AI assistant could list your DEV Community posts, inspect an article, and prepare a new draft without you copying content between browser tabs? That is exactly the kind of workflow the Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables. In this tutorial, you will learn how a DEV.to MCP integration works, how to test it safely, and how to build a small version yourself with TypeScript. The important safety rule comes first: Let the assistant create drafts by default. Publishing should always be an explicit decision.

articles.excerpt: Galaxy Unpacked is right around the corner, and the leaks are rolling in.
articles.body: TL;DR Samsung is expected to launch a new wide foldable, called the Galaxy Z Fold 8, at a Galaxy Unpacked event this week. The wide foldable is tipped to have a 16:10 aspect ratio when closed and a 4:3 aspect ratio when opened, and these leaked images preview what it looks like. See the Galaxy Z Fold 8 ahead of Unpacked in its three retail colorways: Graphite, Cream, and Lavender. Samsung is gearing up to launch new foldables at a Galaxy Unpacked event that’s just days away — and the leaks are heating up. We expect to see three new models, the Galaxy Z Fold 8, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, and the Galaxy Z Flip 8. The base Galaxy Z Fold 8 might be the most exciting, as it will sport a new wide aspect ratio that could excel at handling widescreen media, like videos and games.

articles.excerpt: Robert Downey Jr.'s Doctor Doom makes his true debut (sort of) in this cluttered but colorful preview for the December blockbuster.
articles.body: One thing is for certain: there will absolutely be no shortage of superheroes in Marvel Studios' " Avengers: Doomsday ", as evidenced by this first full trailer that showcases everyone from the Wakandans and the X-Men to the Fantastic Four and Earth's Mightiest Heroes, the Avengers. Plus, we get slivers of a metal-masked Victor Von Doom looming over the mayhem to further whet the appetites of MCU acolytes before the Russo Brothers’ epic lands Dec. 18. "Something's coming," Downey's Doom declares as the trailer rolls out before later stopping Thor's Stormbreaker axe with two fingers. "Something we may not be able to deter.

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy Card announced: Say hello to Samsung’s first credit card in US
articles.excerpt: Samsung says its credit card offers a range of benefits, but no annual fees.
articles.body: Credit: Supplied by Samsung TL;DR Samsung has launched the Samsung Galaxy Card in the US, its first credit card in the market. The company is promising benefits like 5% cash rewards on Samsung purchases and 3% cash rewards on purchases via Samsung Wallet. Select customers can start applying for the Galaxy Card today, while general availability kicks off on July 22. Apple launched the Apple Card way back in 2019, but you were out of luck if you wanted a credit card from an Android phone maker. However, it’s now Samsung’s turn to offer a similar proposition as it just launched the Samsung Galaxy Card in the US. Samsung says the Galaxy Card is the company’s first credit card in the US, adding that it’s a Visa card issued by Barclays US.

articles.title: SpaceX targets July 23 for crucial Starship Flight 13 test launch after abort
articles.excerpt: SpaceX is now targeting Thursday (July 23) for Flight 13 of its giant Starship rocket, after the first launch attempt aborted at the last second on July 16.
articles.body: SpaceX has pushed the next launch of its Starship megarocket back a few more days. Elon Musk 's company is now targeting Thursday (July 23) for Flight 13 of Starship, the giant, fully reusable vehicle it's developing to help get people to the moon and Mars, among other spaceflight tasks. If all goes to plan, the 408-foot-tall (124.4 meters) Starship will lift off from SpaceX 's Starbase site in South Texas on Thursday during a 90-minute window that opens at 6:45 p.m. EDT (2245 GMT; 5:45 p.m. local Texas time). You can watch the action live here at Space.com , courtesy of SpaceX. It will be the second launch attempt for Starship Flight 13 . The first came last Thursday (July 16) and ended in an abort , which occurred just as the giant rocket's 33 first-stage Raptor engines were firing up.

articles.title: One login to rule them all: centralized auth for internal tools with Caddy
articles.excerpt: I am lazy. Not in the "I don't want to work" sense1, but in the "I refuse to solve the same problem twice" sense. The dream: one login, backed by the identity provider the company already uses, and ev…
articles.body: I am lazy. Not in the "I don't want to work" sense 1 , but in the "I refuse to solve the same problem twice" sense. So when I found myself looking at a growing collection of internal tools (each one either completely unprotected or with its own half-baked auth bolted on) I decided it was time to stop and fix it properly. The dream: one login, backed by the identity provider the company already uses, and every new tool just gets two lines in a config file. No user databases to maintain, no password reset flows to implement, no "hey can you add my colleague to the thing" . In our case the company uses Azure AD, so the plan was to put everything behind Caddy and add OAuth2 authentication against the existing Microsoft tenant.
articles.author: Enrico Testori

articles.excerpt: Denshattack! is full of wild moment, but its unexpected ode to Guitar Hero takes the cake.
articles.body: Denshattack! is so chock-full of ridiculous moments that it’s hard to single out one that stands above the rest. I mean, we’re talking about a game where a train hitches a ride on a runaway Ferris wheel, and that seems like a fairly normal occurrence by the end of the adventure. If I had to pick one moment to sell you on it though, it’s Denshattack ’s unexpected musical boss fight.

articles.title: Kevin Feige Denies the MCU Will Reboot With Secret Wars, Points to 2015 Comic as 'Nice Roadmap' for the Future
articles.excerpt: Marvel Studios boss Kevin Feige has confirmed that the MCU will not get a full reboot after Avengers: Secret Wars ties up Phase Six in 2027 – but he does have a few ideas for where to go next.
articles.body: Marvel Studios boss Kevin Feige has confirmed that the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) will not get a full reboot after Avengers: Secret Wars ties up Phase Six in 2027 – but he does have a few ideas for where to go next. The baseball-cap-wearing comic book movie mastermind teased what's next for Marvel's film ambitions during a conversation with Josh Horowitz of Happy, Sad, Confused . There's been some confusion amongst fans when it comes to where films like Avengers: Doomsday and Secret Wars will leave Earth's mightiest heroes upon their premiere, but Feige didn't mince words when asked if next year's team-up film will deliver a reboot. "No," he said, "but I look to the comics, as I always do, in terms of our storytelling.
articles.author: Michael Cripe

articles.title: Dell Outlet Has an Alienware Area-51 RTX 5090 Gaming PC With 64GB of RAM and 4TB SSD for "Just" $5K
articles.excerpt: Comes with the same warranty as buying brand new.
articles.body: Dell Outlet has restocked its inventory of Refurbished "Like New" Alienware Area-51 gaming PCs but one configuration stands out in particular. It's equipped with a GeForce RTX 5090 GPU, 64GB of memory, and 4TB SSD for "just" $5,044 with free shipping and it includes same 1 year warranty as buying brand new . No, it's still not even remotely affordable for most people, but if you've got deep pockets, this is a substantial discount on a beastly Alienware system. If you look at the prices of the individual components in today's market, it's (sadly) not as ridiculous as your initial sticker shock might lead you to believe.

articles.body: NASA’s SpaceX Crew-13 members are pictured in their pressure suits seated inside a mockup Dragon spacecraft during a preflight training session at the company’s headquarters in Hawthorne, California. From left are Roscosmos Sergey Teteryatnikov, NASA astronauts Luke Delaney and Jessica Watkins, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Joshua Kutryk. Credit: SpaceX NASA and its partners will discuss the upcoming crew rotation mission to the International Space Station during a pair of news conferences on Monday, Aug. 3, from the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. Mission leadership will provide an overview of NASA’s SpaceX Crew‑13 mission at 12 p.m. EDT. Next, crew members will discuss their training and mission preparations at 2 p.m.

articles.title: Scientists discover mystery molecule on Pluto and Saturn's moon Titan: 'We cannot say what it is'
articles.excerpt: Despite being separated by billions of miles, Saturn's moon Titan and the dwarf planet Pluto share an unexplained surface signature.
articles.body: Separated by billions of miles and shaped by very different environments, Saturn's smoggy moon Titan and the dwarf planet Pluto have, on paper, little in common. Titan is a geologically active world cloaked in an atmosphere denser than Earth's, its landscape shaped by seas of liquid methane and vast dunes built from organic particles that rain out of its skies. Pluto, by contrast, is a frigid outpost with a tenuous atmosphere hovering above an icy, frozen surface. Yet, observations from the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have revealed these two disparate worlds share an identical, unexplained infrared signature on their surfaces — a sign that similar chemistry may be unfolding on both worlds despite their stark differences, scientists say.
articles.author: Sharmila Kuthunur

articles.title: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration
articles.excerpt: An aircraft powered by a megawatt-class hybrid-electric engine developed in collaboration with NASA and built by GE Aerospace, demonstrated flight of an innovation that can inform new generations of f…
articles.body: 4 min read Preparations for Next Moonwalk Simulations Underway (and Underwater) A modified Saab 340B aircraft in flight powered in part by a hybrid electric system built by GE Aerospace, along with NASA, BETA Technologies, and Boeing. GE Aerospace An aircraft powered by a megawatt-class hybrid-electric engine developed in collaboration with NASA and built by GE Aerospace, demonstrated flight of an innovation that can inform new generations of fuel-saving aircraft power systems. Mounted to a Saab 340B aircraft, the engine flew at Farnborough International Air Show in the United Kingdom. It was the public debut of a system that has in recent months made historic test flights, becoming the first hybrid electric-powered aircraft to fly above 30,000 feet.

articles.title: 50 years after Viking 1's historic Mars landing, NASA is looking to the Red Planet's skies
articles.excerpt: NASA's Viking 1 mission blazed a crucial trail with its Mars landing on July 20, 1976. Now, half a century later, that trail leads up into the Red Planet's skies.
articles.body: Half a century after first putting hardware down on Mars, NASA is looking to explore the Red Planet's skies. On July 20, 1976, the Viking 1 lander touched down in the western reaches of Chryse Planitia (the "Golden Plain"), a large circular landform that lies 22.5 degrees north of the Martian equator. It was the first-ever Mars landing for NASA. Viking 1, along with its twin Viking 2, went on to conduct the first fully successful mission on the Red Planet's surface. (The Soviet Union's Mars 3 probe survived its touchdown attempt on Dec. 2, 1971 but died less than two minutes later.) And those missions have left a rich and intriguing legacy. The first color image ever captured on the surface of Mars. NASA's Viking 1 lander took this photo on July 21, 1976.
articles.author: Mike Wall

articles.title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
articles.excerpt: Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR…
articles.body: Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated with a suite of generative AI tools. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own Firefly AI models. Adobe describes the new "AI Playground" tooling suite as an experiment and says there's a button that allows users to opt out and continue using the app as before. Free access to the suite with no sign-on requirement is being tested with a small percentage of Indigo users over the next few weeks. "Our goal is to learn how people … Read the full story at The Verge.
